Mid Product Designer
Lunar Design Systems — Empowering teams to design beautiful, scalable digital experiences that connect people
to what matters most

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-friendly)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$120,000 - $155,000 USD annually

The Opportunity

Join Lunar Design Systems as a Mid Product Designer and take ownership of critical user experiences within our
design platform. You'll collaborate cross-functionally with engineers, product managers, and design systems
specialists to evolve our core design tools. This role offers the opportunity to ship meaningful features, mentor
junior designers, and contribute to design direction while maintaining high standards for usability and accessibility.
You'll work on problems that impact thousands of designers daily.

About Lunar Design Systems

Lunar Design Systems is a fast-growing design infrastructure company that helps enterprise teams build consistent,

accessible digital products at scale. Founded in 2020, we provide a comprehensive platform that combines design
systems management, component libraries, and collaborative design tooling. Our customers include Fortune 500

companies across fintech, healthcare, and e-commerce sectors. We're backed by leading venture capital firms and
are headquartered in San Francisco with a remote-friendly culture.

Day-to-Day

Design user interfaces and interaction flows for new product features using Figma, translating requirements into
high-fidelity prototypes with clear interaction specifications

Build and maintain components within our internal design system, ensuring consistency across the product and

documenting design decisions for the broader team

Conduct usability research and user testing to validate design solutions; synthesize findings and iterate designs

based on data and feedback

Collaborate with engineering teams to ensure design implementation fidelity; participate in design reviews and
provide constructive feedback on peer work

Contribute to the evolution of our design system and component library; identify gaps and opportunities for
standardization that improve team velocity

Create detailed design documentation and interaction specifications using Figma annotations, design tokens, and
hand-off materials for developers

Participate in cross-functional product planning meetings; contribute design perspective to feature prioritization
and roadmap decisions

 Mentor and provide feedback to junior designers on design fundamentals, design systems thinking, and
collaboration best practices

You Have

2-5 years of professional product design experience, with a strong portfolio demonstrating design process,
prototyping, and shipped digital products

Expertise in Figma and proficiency with design tools including Protopie or similar high-fidelity prototyping
platforms

Strong understanding of design systems principles, component-based design, and design tokens; experience
working with or building design systems

Demonstrated knowledge of interaction design patterns, information architecture, and usability principles with

ability to articulate design rationale

Excellent communication and collaboration skills; experience working in cross-functional teams with engineers
and product managers

Portfolio or case studies showing end-to-end design work from research through implementation, with focus on
complex enterprise or B2B interfaces

Bonus

Experience with design system tools such as Storybook, Zeroheight, or comparable documentation platforms

Familiarity with web technologies and front-end fundamentals (HTML/CSS) to better communicate with

engineering partners

Background in accessibility (WCAG, inclusive design) with demonstrated commitment to accessible product
design

Experience with user research methodologies and usability testing tools like UserTesting, Maze, or Hotjar

Our Stack

Figma   Protopie   Design Systems (component architecture)   Design Tokens   Storybook   Zeroheight

Adobe Creative Suite (XD, Illustrator)   CSS fundamentals   Interaction Design principles

Cross-functional collaboration tools (Jira, Notion, Slack)

What We Offer

Competitive health insurance including medical, dental, and vision coverage with employer contribution

Unlimited paid time off plus 10 company holidays and flexible work schedule

Professional development budget ($2,500 annually) for conferences, courses, and design tools

Equity (stock options) as part of comprehensive compensation package

Monthly $150 wellness stipend covering gym membership, mental health services, or wellness apps

Fully stocked office kitchen and regular team social events

Home office stipend of $500 for remote-first setup and ergonomic equipment

 Parental leave and family support benefits (12 weeks paid for primary caregiver, 6 weeks for partner)

Apply

Apply through our careers page at lunar.design/careers or email your portfolio link, resume, and a brief cover letter
explaining your design philosophy to careers@lunar.design. Portfolio reviews typically take 1-2 weeks. We review
applications on a rolling basis.

Familiarity with web technologies and front-end fundamentals (HTML/CSS) to better communicate with
Background in accessibility (WCAG, inclusive design) with demonstrated commitment to accessible product
Experience with user research methodologies and usability testing tools like UserTesting, Maze, or Hotjar
